bottom_line: Add 'read design doc → verify findings → then edit' directive to code-review-impl prompts to eliminate spurious scope-violation verdicts and noisy fix-cycle diagnostics that cost 1 fix cycle and 3 redundant review runs.
coverage_notes: S01 (full), S02-run1+run2 (full), S03 (full), S04-run1+run2 (full), S05 (full), S06-run1+run2 (full), S07-run1+run2 (empty logs noted), S08 (full), S09-fix1+run3 (full; run1 empty), S10-S14 (full). No tarball archive available — item is active. DB telemetry: item-status JSON only; no direct DB queries made.

findings.title: Code-review agents misattribute worktree-diff pollution to the current step
evidence: ai-dev/logs/I-00116_S04_run1.log:7 — "F1 CRITICAL step_monitor.py is modified in this worktree but S03 was only allowed to touch fix_cycle.py and batch_manager.py. The scope boundary is violated"
ai-dev/logs/I-00116_S06_run1.log:10 — "CRITICAL Finding: Three daemon files...are also modified in this worktree. They are out of scope for S05/S06"  (also seen in S04)
findings.recommendation: Add a directive to agents/*/code-review-impl.md instructing reviewers: before flagging a scope-violation, confirm the change exists in git log for that step specifically. Alternatively: explicitly name which prior steps' changes are pre-existing in the worktree so reviewers know to discount them.
findings.pros: Eliminates ~1 wasted run per affected code-review step; prevents spurious fail verdicts on multi-step items.
findings.cons: Requires regenerating 3 files; adds a pre-check step to every code-review agent's diagnostic flow.
findings.if_we_dont: Fix-cycle waste continues on every multi-step item where multiple backend steps modify overlapping files in the same worktree.
findings.paste_prompt: /iw-new-cr Add scope-verification preamble to code-review-impl prompts: before flagging scope-violation, check git log for the current step. Analyzed in I-00116 (see ai-dev/active/I-00116/reports/I-00116_self_assess_report.md). Target: agents/*/code-review-impl.md. Effort: S.

findings.title: Fix-cycle diagnostic hypothesis is used as truth rather than verified against the design doc
evidence: ai-dev/active/I-00116/fix-cycles/I-00116_S09_FIX_cycle1_prompt.md:12 — "Diagnostic Hypothesis — Findings to Address: Process exited without reporting completion (PID dead)"
ai-dev/logs/I-00116_S09_fix1.log:7 — "Diagnostic: Process exited without reporting completion (PID dead)"
ai-dev/logs/I-00116_S09_fix1.log:11 — "Changes applied: Added # noqa: SLF001 comments..."  (actual fault was lint failures, not PID recovery)
findings.recommendation: Update the fix-cycle template to add a mandatory 'verify diagnostic' step before editing: the agent must confirm the reported finding is actually present in the code. The existing template warns 'the spec wins on conflict' but the diagnostic is being accepted as-ground-truth. Enforce: investigate → confirm → fix.
findings.pros: Fixes the root cause of noisy diagnostics wasting cycles; aligns fix-cycle behavior with existing pre-fix procedure.
findings.cons: Requires template update; may slow fix-cycle agents slightly on true-positives.
findings.if_we_dont: Fix cycles apply correct fixes for wrong reasons (S09) or risk applying wrong fixes when the diagnostic is genuinely wrong.
findings.paste_prompt: /iw-new-cr Make fix-cycle template enforce diagnostic verification before editing: agent must confirm finding is present before applying fix. Analyzed in I-00116 (see ai-dev/active/I-00116/reports/I-00116_self_assess_report.md). Target: templates/design/FixCycle_Template.md. Effort: M.

findings.title: SLF001 lint violation bloomed through S03→S04→S05→S06→S09-fix (5+ steps before resolution)
evidence: ai-dev/logs/I-00116_S04_run1.log:15 — "make lint fails with 2 SLF001 private-member-access violations (fc._count_review_relaunches, fc._transition_item_to_failed_for_loop)"
ai-dev/logs/I-00116_S05_run1.log:30 — "make lint: pass...pre-existing SLF001 warnings in batch_manager.py unrelated to S05"
ai-dev/logs/I-00116_S06_run2.log:20 — "make lint has a pre-existing SLF001 warning from S03 in batch_manager.py"
ai-dev/logs/I-00116_S09_fix1.log:11 — "Added # noqa: SLF001 comments to the two private-member accesses"
findings.recommendation: Require Backend step prompts to run targeted `make lint` against the two files they modified after each edit. Alternatively: document whether the project convention is (a) public helper functions (no `_` prefix), or (b) private helpers with per-call `# noqa: SLF001` annotations, and enforce consistently. S03 introduced `_get_max_review_relaunches` as private; a public `get_max_review_relaunches` would eliminate all downstream `noqa` annotations. Option (b) is faster to implement.
findings.target_file: orch/daemon/fix_cycle.py  (OR: if public-convention chosen: orch/daemon/batch_manager.py + design doc fix; if noqa-convention chosen: just annotate batch_manager.py calls immediately in the same step that introduces them)
findings.pros: Prevents lint waste through the review chain; aligns with existing pattern in scope_overlap._matches / fc._latest_failure_reason.
findings.cons: Requires operator decision: public-helper vs. noqa-annotation convention; affects all future DAEMON cap implementations.
findings.if_we_dont: Any future Backend step adding cross-module private helper calls will trigger the same 3-5 step lint propagation before resolution.
findings.effort_lines: ~2 items: decide + document convention; add lint check to Backend Post-Edit Gate
findings.paste_prompt: /iw-new-incident Add targeted lint check to Backend step Post-Edit Gate for cross-module private helper calls to prevent SLF001 bloomed violations. Analyzed in I-00116 (see ai-dev/active/I-00116/reports/I-00116_self_assess_report.md). Target: Backend prompt template. Effort: M.

findings.title: Test-agent log capture gap (S07): 0-byte run logs for successful executions
evidence: ai-dev/logs/I-00116_S07_run1.log — 0 bytes (empty; S07 executions produced no stdout captured to log)
findings.recommendation: Investigate why agents using tool-use mode produce no stdout-to-log capture. Ensure the executor writes a summary log entry (at minimum: step ID, agent, timestamp, exit code) even when the agent bypasses shell stdout. Add a post-step hook that writes the result contract to ai-dev/logs/ for every step run.
findings.target_file: executor/  (shell scripts that capture agent output and write logs — worktree_commit.sh or agent harness config)
findings.pros: Enables full post-hoc analysis for tool-use-only agent executions; closes a blind spot for iw-item-analyze skill.
findings.cons: Requires executor/infrastructure changes; may produce noisy empty logs for fast steps if not carefully scoped.
findings.if_we_dont: iw-item-analyze skill will have incomplete log coverage for any steps where agents bypass stdout, making future self-assessments unreliable.
findings.effort_lines: ~50–100 lines, 1 executor script change + integration test
findings.paste_prompt: /iw-new-incident Investigate and fix test-agent log capture gap: successful S07 had 0-byte logs in ai-dev/logs/. Analyzed in I-00116 (see ai-dev/active/I-00116/reports/I-00116_self_assess_report.md). Target: executor/worktree_commit.sh. Effort: L.
